title: Old-age security motives, labor markets, and farm family fertility in antebellum American
mechanism: old-age security motive and labor markets
reason: Empirical study of old-age-security motives on farm family fertility in antebellum America

title: Risk and Insurance: Perspectives on Fertility and Agrarian Change in India and Bangladesh
mechanism: risk and insurance value of children (old-age/security)
reason: Empirical comparison of insurance/security demand for children explaining fertility differences in South Asia

title: Pensions and Fertility: Micro-Economic Evidence
mechanism: pensions / old-age security
reason: Micro-economic evidence on pensions and fertility, forward empirical estimate.
ra_note: not a paper -- just an appendix

title: Data and Code for: The Old-Age Security Motive for Fertility: Evidence from the Extension of Social Pensions in Namibia
mechanism: extension of social pensions / old-age security
reason: Quasi-experimental evidence from Namibia's social-pension extension showing pensions substantially reduce fertility; the core primary estimand.
ra_note: not a paper -- just data & code

title: The Economic Old Age Security Value of Children in the Philippines and Taiwan in the Middle 1970'S.
mechanism: old-age economic security value of children / support expectations
reason: Value-of-Children surveys show old-age support expectations significantly raise fertility preferences in Philippines/Taiwan; forward empirical OAS-motive effect on fertility.

title: Old Age Security Value of Children and Development: Cross-National Evidence
mechanism: perceived old-age-security value of children
reason: Cross-national Value of Children evidence linking OAS value of children to fertility, forward direction.

title: Children, support in old age and social insurance in rural China
outcome: demand for children / son preference / sex ratio
mechanism: rural pension program substituting for children as old-age support
reason: DiD/observational evidence that rural pensions substitute for children in old age and reduce demand for children, forward on fertility.

title: FERTILITY REDUCTION AS A METHOD OF POPULATION ADAPTATION TO PENSION SYSTEM DYNAMIC CHANGE
mechanism: retirement-age increase lengthening women's employment period
reason: Empirical analysis of Russia's retirement-age reform on the birth rate plus survey evidence; forward pension-policy -> fertility estimate.

title: INCOME, SOCIAL SECURITY AND CHINESE FAMILIES’ “TWO-CHILD” DECISIONS: EVIDENCE FROM URBAN RESIDENTS’ FERTILITY INTENTIONS
mechanism: social-security substitution effect on demand for children
reason: CGSS analysis finds endowment insurance/social security lowers urban residents' fertility intentions; forward empirical estimate.

title: Fertility decisions and the norm of intergenerational support to aging parents
mechanism: weakening filial-responsibility (old-age support) norm reduces value of children, lowering fertility
reason: life-cycle model estimated on Indonesian data with counterfactuals quantifying norm's effect on fertility

title: The effects of education, health, and social security on fertility in developing countries
mechanism: social security reduces old-age reliance on children
reason: Analyzes and estimates effects of education, health and social security on fertility in developing countries; forward social-security effect on fertility.

title: High Fertility as Risk-Insurance
mechanism: children as old-age/risk insurance; social security as substitute
reason: Historical and cross-sectional empirical test of the old-age/risk-insurance motive for high fertility and whether social security is needed for fertility decline.

title: Level of Urbanization, Social Security Satisfaction and Fertility Intentions
mechanism: social security satisfaction / provision
reason: Estimates forward effect of social-security satisfaction on fertility intentions (OLS/ordered probit) in China.

title: Does parents’ retirement influence the fertility intentions of subsequent generations among internal migrant populations? Evidence from China
mechanism: parental retirement -> intergenerational wealth transfer (partly offset by grandparental childcare)
reason: Fuzzy-RD on mandatory retirement estimates forward effect of parental retirement/pension on offspring fertility intentions.

title: The Security Dem and for Children in Peninsular Malaysia.
mechanism: old-age-security / insurance demand for children; pension introduction
reason: theory+empirical test that pension introduction reduces fertility (security demand for children)

title: Social Security Expectation,Birth Rate and Population Quality
mechanism: social security expectation crowding out old-age-support motive
reason: Diamond model plus provincial panel test finding social security expectation inhibits fertility; forward empirical estimate on fertility.

title: Fertility, contraceptive use and intra-familial transfers in rural Malawi
mechanism: old-age support via intra-familial (upward) transfers in absence of social security
reason: Directly tests the old-age-support/wealth-flows motive: whether fertility responds to net transfer direction where social security is absent.

title: Influence of women’s workforce participation and pensions on total fertility rate: a theoretical and econometric study
mechanism: pensions and women's workforce participation
reason: Theoretical-and-econometric study of the effect of pensions (and female labor participation) on TFR; provides a forward empirical estimate on fertility.

title: Old-age security, religious celibacy, and aggregate fertility in a Tibetan population
mechanism: old-age security motive (celibacy/support)
reason: directly analyzes old-age security and aggregate fertility in a Tibetan population

title: Women's status and fertility in developing countries: Son preference and economic security
mechanism: value of children as old-age security assets / son preference
reason: Cross-national empirical test of the old-age economic-security value of children on fertility (forward).

title: Rearing cost, endowment insurance and fertility desire
mechanism: old-age (endowment) insurance substitutes for children as old-age support
reason: Estimates negative effect of old-age endowment insurance on fertility desire using CFPS (OLG-motivated), forward OAS-crowdout cell.
